sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.27 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: Is it true that the group$SL_n(q)$contains, for sufficiently large$q$, a diagonal matrix which is not contained in any proper irreducible subgroup of$SL_n(q)$with the exception of block-monomial ones? For$n=2,\, 3$the answer is known to be affirmative (V.\,M.\,Levchuk, in: Some problems of the theory of groups and rings, Inst. of Physics SO AN SSSR, Krasnoyarsk, 1973 (Russian)). Similar problems a...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
